react.answer:
## Building a File Explorer (tree view) in React **Mental model:** A file system is a tree of nodes (`type: 'folder' | 'file'`, optional `children`). Render it recursively. Folders toggle open/closed; files are leaves. Track expanded folders in a `Set` of ids so the open/closed state survives re-renders without mutating the data. ### Step 1 — Node shape + recursive component ```jsx function Node({ node }) { if (node.type === 'file') return
📄 {node.name}
; return ; } ``` ### Step 2 — Expanded set (lifted state) ```jsx const [open, setOpen] = useState(new Set()); const toggle = (id) => setOpen((s) => { const n = new Set(s); n.has(id) ? n.delete(id) : n.add(id); return n; }); ``` ### Step 3 — Render folder + children when open ```jsx
toggle(node.id)}>{open.has(node.id) ? '📂' : '📁'} {node.name}
{open.has(node.id) && node.children.map((c) => )} ``` ### Step 4 — Indent by depth Pass a `depth` prop and use it for left padding. ```jsx
…
``` **Edge cases** | Case | Handling | | :-- | :-- | | Empty folder | Renders with no children when open. | | Deep trees | Recursion handles any depth. | | Click bubbling | Stop propagation if files have their own actions. | **Interview tip:** Holding expanded ids in a `Set` (lifted above the recursion) rather than a boolean per node keeps the tree data immutable and the open/closed state in one place — the cleaner design interviewers look for.

vue.answer:
## Building a File Explorer (tree view) in Vue 3 **Mental model:** A tree of nodes (`folder`/`file`, optional `children`) rendered by a self-referencing component. Folders toggle open/closed; files are leaves. Hold expanded ids in a shared `Set` so the data stays immutable and state lives in one place. ### Step 1 — Recursive component ```vue ``` ### Step 2 — Expanded set at the root ```js const open = ref(new Set(['root'])); function toggle(id) { const n = new Set(open.value); n.has(id) ? n.delete(id) : n.add(id); open.value = n; } ``` ### Step 3 — Render folder vs file ```vue
{{ open.has(node.id) ? '📂' : '📁' }} {{ node.name }}
📄 {{ node.name }}
``` ### Step 4 — Indent by depth ```vue
…
``` **Edge cases** | Case | Handling | | :-- | :-- | | Empty folder | No children rendered when open. | | Deep trees | Self-referencing component. | | State location | Single root `Set`, passed down read-only. | **Interview tip:** Lifting the expanded `Set` to the root and passing it down (with a `toggle` event bubbling up) keeps the node data immutable — cleaner than a boolean baked into each node.

angular.answer:
## Building a File Explorer (tree view) in Angular **Mental model:** A tree of nodes (`folder`/`file`, optional `children`) rendered by a self-referencing component. Folders toggle; files are leaves. A shared `Set` of expanded ids (passed down) keeps node data immutable. Classic `NgModule` setup, no `?.`/`??` (the build rejects them). ### Step 1 — Recursive component ```html ``` ### Step 2 — Expanded set at the root ```typescript open = new Set(['root']); toggle(id: string): void { if (this.open.has(id)) { this.open.delete(id); } else { this.open.add(id); } } ``` ### Step 3 — Folder vs file ```html
{{ open.has(node.id) ? '📂' : '📁' }} {{ node.name }}
📄 {{ node.name }}
``` ### Step 4 — Indent by depth ```html
…
``` **Edge cases** | Case | Handling | | :-- | :-- | | Empty folder | No children when open. | | Deep trees | Self-referencing component. | | State location | Root `Set`, mutated only at the root. | **Interview tip:** A component using its own selector is Angular's tree recursion; bubbling `toggle` up to a single root `Set` mutation keeps the node data clean and the state centralized.
